title: PM Modi flags off India's first hydrogen train; check route, timings & how it works
content: India’s first hydrogen train is a 10-coach passenger offering. According to Indian Railways, globally the few operational or under testing hydrogen trains have 2-3 passenger coaches, making India’s first hydrogen train the longest of its type in the world.

title: 'If it fails, I'll return as a ghost': Wangchuk ahead of July 20 Parliament march
content: Activist Sonam Wangchuk continues his hunger strike for the 20th day. He plans to join a march to Parliament on July 20. The Cockroach Janta Party supports Wangchuk and the ongoing protests. Political leaders have expressed concern over Wangchuk's deteriorating health. The protests demand action regarding alleged irregularities in the NEET examination.

title: Hegseth shares photo of Chabahar port tower collapsing amid strikes in Iran
content: Chabahar port has been a repeated target of American airstrikes. Iranian state media acknowledged a third round of strikes on the facility without immediately acknowledging the tower’s collapse. Iran, however, described the tower as overseeing commercial traffic into the port. However, Iran’s paramilitary Revolutionary Guard also operates at ports across the country.

title: India coach gives blunt 'no bridge needed' response on Kohli-Gambhir rift
content: Ahead of the first ODI at Edgbaston, Kohli was seen batting in the nets while Gambhir watched from close range without speaking to him. Kotak, meanwhile, was spotted having a conversation with the former India captain.

title: 3 students killed, 4 critical after train collides with school van in Murshidabad
content: A local train struck a school vehicle at an open level crossing, killing four people. The collision occurred Friday morning between Karnasubarna station and Gobindapur rail gate. Four students and the vehicle driver were hospitalized in critical condition after the incident. Eyewitnesses alleged the rail gate remained open, leading to the accident. Investigations are underway by police and railway authorities into the cause.

title: Winners will make the hard choices: Microsoft's security chief to employees after layoffs
content: Microsoft's new security chief Hayete Gallot has replaced senior executives, merged engineering teams, and cut hundreds of jobs to push the company toward AI-powered cybersecurity, The Information reports. In an internal memo, Gallot told staff the industry is being reimagined and rewards firms that make hard choices early. Microsoft is chasing AI security spending flowing to rivals OpenAI and Anthropic.

title: Wife can't use RTI to access husband's ITRs for maintenance case: Delhi HC
content: Justice Purushaindra Kumar Kaurav delivered the judgment in a writ petition filed by the husband challenging an order passed by the Central Information Commission directing disclosure of his net taxable income from the financial year 2007-08 onwards. The Central Information Commission, by its order dated 22.07.2021, had directed disclosure of the husband’s taxable income details.

title: Jeff Bezos donates $10m to LA that city's mayor says is about investing in culture
content: Jeff Bezos' Earth Fund has granted $10.1 million for the Watts Towers Arts Center Campus. This investment will help transform the campus ahead of the 2028 Olympic and Paralympic Games. The project adds over 30,000 square feet of usable park space and cooling features. New recreational and gathering areas will also be introduced as part of the revitalization. This funding advances commitments to expanding green space and community resilience.

title: 6,000 penguin dives reveal how sea ice can make prey harder to reach
content: In recent times, penguins face challenges in sourcing food due to altered prey behaviors. As krill and fish migrate deeper or spread out to elude predators, penguins must dive greater depths and swim longer distances in search of sustenance. Thus, it's crucial for conservation initiatives to take into account these changing hunting dynamics in our evolving oceans.

title: US govt's new rule: Visa, green card can be denied if likely to need public assistance
content: The US government has revised its public charge immigration rule. Immigrants may be denied entry if they are likely to depend on public assistance. This new regulation restores broader discretion to immigration officers for assessments. The policy change will officially take effect on September 18, 2026. USCIS also updated rules regarding signatures on immigration benefit requests.

title: House hunting? These tips can help you avoid AI rental scams
content: In an era of advanced technology, online rental scams have become more cunning, leveraging artificial intelligence to deceive unsuspecting tenants. Fraudsters craft fake property listings and convincingly impersonate landlords. To safeguard against these scams, it's crucial for renters to be cautious of unusually low rents and landlords who refuse inspections. Always conduct in-person visits and confirm ownership before making any financial commitments, opting for secure payment methods to mitigate risks.

title: 'Nothing was handed to Argentina': Messi unloads on favouritism claims
content: Argentina came from 1-0 down after the 85th minute to beat England 2-1 in Wednesday's semifinal and book a place in the final, where they will face Spain on Sunday.

title: Gigi Hadid reportedly deepened Blake Lively's fallout with Taylor Swift
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce kept the drama away from their New York City wedding as they invited pretty much everyone from Hollywood and the NFL but Blake Lively and Ryan Reynolds, sparking chaos among many. While this has sparked a mixed reaction among fans, it seems supermodel Gigi Hadid might have made things worse for Blake Lively.

title: Who is Fátima Bosch? The Miss Universe linked to Jude Bellingham rumors
content: Miss Universe 2025 winner Fatima Bosch has attracted worldwide attention after social media users associated her with England national football team star Jude Bellingham. Bosch has denied meeting her, but the rumors have sparked a lot of interest in her life, her education, her career and her amazing journey from Mexico to becoming one of the world’s most recognizable beauty queens.

title: Marcello Hernandez's Tiger Woods joke steals the spotlight at the ESPYs
content: Marcello Hernandez delivered some of the biggest moments at the 2026 ESPYs with jokes aimed at Tiger Woods and Bill Belichick. Hernandez referenced Woods’ ongoing DUI case and poked fun at Belichick’s relationship with Jordon Hudson. The comedian’s sharp monologue drew strong reactions from the audience and became one of the night’s main talking points.

title: The great Indian flip: People on roads, bikes on footpaths
content: Indian footpaths are now unsafe for pedestrians due to encroaching vehicles. Delivery riders and two-wheelers use pavements as shortcuts, creating hazards. Congestion and poor enforcement exacerbate the problem across major cities. This situation discourages walking, impacting public health and urban sustainability. Engineering, enforcement, and behavioral changes are needed to reclaim pedestrian spaces.

title: How chance discovery of Rs 40,000 in loo helped blow lid off Ayodhya Ram temple theft
content: A chance discovery of concealed cash led to an investigation into donation theft. Alerted by a gatekeeper, temple trustees initiated an internal inquiry into the matter. Police launched a probe and recovered nearly eighty lakh rupees from multiple locations. Accused individuals provided disclosures which aided further recoveries of stolen money. A special investigation team is now reconstructing the entire sequence of events.

title: Masterpiece or Overrated: 'The Odyssey' Twitter reviews divide Nolan fans
content: Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey' has premiered in IMAX theaters, sparking audience debate. Many fans praise its stunning visuals and Nolan's ambitious direction. However, some viewers criticize the film's pacing and storytelling choices. Dialogue and acting performances also received mixed reactions from moviegoers. Matt Damon's portrayal of Odysseus, however, earned widespread acclaim from many.

title: Wife can't be denied 'stridhan' just because she has no bills or receipts: Chhattisgarh HC
content: Chhattisgarh high court ruled a wife need not produce bills for stridhan. Husband's appeal was dismissed regarding his wife's stridhan articles. The court noted receipts are typically kept by the husband's family after marriage. Absence of documents does not negate the wife's claim for her belongings. The husband must return the stridhan or pay two lakh rupees.

title: Rupee rises 14 paise in early trade as Dalal Street cheers; oil adds limited gains
content: The rupee opened stronger against the US dollar on Friday, gaining fourteen paise in early trade. Upbeat equity markets provided support for the domestic currency's early gains. However, sustained foreign fund outflows and firm crude oil prices capped further appreciation. Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East kept investors cautious towards emerging market currencies. The rupee is expected to trade within a narrow range with a downside bias.

title: 'Hasn't scored in two games': India coach's verdict on Rohit Sharma amid poor form
content: Rohit's struggles were visible in the second ODI in Cardiff on Thursday, where England won by four wickets to level the three-match series 1-1. The opener never looked settled, scoring just 10 runs off 18 balls by the end of the Powerplay even as India raced to 61/1. He eventually made 26 off 47 balls before being dismissed while attempting a sweep against Will Jacks.

title: Joe Root surpasses Virat Kohli's ODI record with unbeaten 99 against India
content: Joe Root's unbeaten 99 guided England to a four-wicket victory over India.  This win leveled the three-match series at one game apiece.  Root absorbed early pressure and anchored the innings with a composed knock.  He overtook Virat Kohli in successful ODI run chase averages.  England's bowlers restricted India after a threatening partnership.

title: Woman waiting at Mangaluru bus stop hacked to death; one-sided love angle emerges
content: A tragic incident occurred when a 21-year-old woman was brutally attacked near the BC Road bus stand. Identified as Lavanya, she succumbed to her injuries and was pronounced dead at Bantwal Government Hospital. Investigators have identified Chetan, a distant relative with unresolved affections, as the alleged perpetrator, and efforts to apprehend him are ongoing.

title: Man driving Mercedes can't call Rs 20,000 maintenance for wife excessive: Karnataka HC
content: The Karnataka High Court dismissed a husband's divorce appeal based on cruelty and desertion. The court also upheld an order for him to pay interim maintenance. A man owning a Mercedes-Benz cannot claim this payment is excessive. The husband failed to prove cruelty allegations against his wife. The court found the wife had a reasonable cause to live separately.

title: Rachit breaks silence on October wedding rumours with Huma
content: Rachit Singh has denied wedding rumours with Huma Qureshi, saying, "It's not happening!" Speculation grew after the two attended a wedding reception together and made frequent public appearances. Rachit, also an acting coach for a decade, spoke about working with Huma and her brother Saqib Saleem, and shared his goal of acting lifelong.

title: NEET UG 2026 cut-off released alongside results; Check category-wise qualifying marks, percentiles
content: The NTA has announced the NEET UG 2026 cut-off along with the examination results, outlining the category-wise qualifying percentiles and marks required for medical admissions. With over 11.21 lakh candidates qualifying, eligible aspirants can now participate in the counselling process. The qualifying marks have increased compared to last year, reflecting stronger overall performance in the examination.

title: Aamir Khan dismisses '3 Idiots' link to Sonam Wangchuk; voices concern as his fast continues
content: Aamir Khan addressed the popular belief about his character Phunsukh Wangdu. He stated that the '3 Idiots' character was not based on Sonam Wangchuk. Khan clarified this misconception during a London Film Festival interaction. He also expressed deep concern for Wangchuk's ongoing hunger strike. The actor hopes for a positive and healthy resolution to the protest.

title: Hydrogen train, Rs 25K cr in projects: PM Modi's agenda in Haryana, Chandigarh & Punjab
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will launch development projects exceeding twenty-four thousand eight hundred crore rupees.  These initiatives span highways, railways, healthcare, and education infrastructure across three regions.  The visit includes flagging off India's first hydrogen-powered train from Jind to Sonipat.  He will also inaugurate completed projects and lay foundation stones for new developments.  This significant rollout aims to boost regional connectivity and public services.

title: Hyderabad school 'Kalma' homework row: NHRC issues notice to Telangana govt
content: The National Human Rights Commission has issued a notice to the Telangana government. This action follows a complaint alleging compulsory Islamic religious lessons and prayers at a private college. The institution reportedly assigned these religious tasks as mandatory homework for all students. The commission seeks a detailed report from state authorities regarding these allegations. Further information on this developing matter is currently awaited.

title: NEET UG 2026 AIR 1 Aryan Gupta shares success mantra, advice for aspirants
content: The NTA has announced the NEET UG 2026 results, with over 11.21 lakh candidates qualifying for medical admissions. Punjab's Aryan Gupta, who secured a joint AIR 1 with 715 marks, credited his success to 16–17 hours of daily study and perseverance. Aspiring to become an oncologist, he urged future aspirants to keep working hard and never lose hope.

title: 'China stole data of 220m US voters': Trump blames Beijing as he releases files on 2020 polls
content: President Donald Trump alleged China compromised 220 million American voter files. He announced declassification of intelligence documents regarding election interference. Trump claimed intelligence officials concealed this information from him and Congress. He stated that election systems are vulnerable and easily compromised. This announcement comes as Republicans prepare for upcoming midterm elections.

title: Top stocks to buy: Stock recommendations for today - check list
content: Stock market recommendations: Adani Power, and IndusInd Bank - Bajaj Broking Research has recommended these two stocks to buy today, July 17, 2026, with a one-month horizon for the target price to be achieved. Let’s take a look:

title: Vivek Oberoi reveals he invested in over 30 companies, exited 11 with returns of up to 300%
content: While many know Vivek Oberoi as an actor, he has quietly built a significant presence in the investment world. In a recent interview, the actor spoke about his journey from learning the value of money as a child to creating a family office focused on generational wealth, impact investing and backing promising entrepreneurs.

title: Rohit Sharma no more automatic selection after Lord's ODI
content: The Indian team management and selectors had initially considered giving Yashasvi Jaiswal a full series in England but opted to persist with Rohit Sharma, hoping he would rediscover his form. Sources indicate that both the selection committee and team management have conveyed their views on Rohit's future to the BCCI. It remains to be seen whether the board's leadership or the selectors will now initiate discussions with him.

title: Give consumers option between 100% petrol and ethanol-blended petrol, Aaditya Thackeray writes to PM Modi
content: Mr. Thackeray said lakhs of vehicle owners, particularly from the middle class and youth, have expressed concerns over the rising percentage of ethanol in petrol

title: Jantar Mantar protest LIVE: 'Will stay alive till July 20 at any cost', says Sonam Wangchuk
content: Wangchuk has lost over 9 kg after starting hunger strike and his body is consuming muscles and his organs may be next, says doctor

title: Ground water levels fall in 18 districts; Telangana releases agriculture contingency plan for Vanakalam 2026
content: Majority of the major, medium, and minor irrigation reservoirs in Telangana are presently at, or very close to, their Minimum Draw Down Level (MDDL)

title: Telangana reservoirs levels constrict irrigation usage; ground water in State may plummet from 9.46 mgbl to 11.01 mgbl
content: Farmers in Telangana urged to harvest rainwater, opt less water-consuming crops. Agriculture department lists alternate crops that can be taken up in the weather conditions which changed under the impact of El Nino.

title: Train collides with school van in West Bengal's Murshidabad, at least three dead
content: Two of the three deceased are minors; the injured survivors have admitted to Murshidabad Medical College and Hospital

title: 16th batch of over 4600 pilgrims leave for Amarnath cave from Jammu
content: More than 3.45 lakh pilgrims have paid obeisance to Lord Shiva at the 3,880-metre-high cave shrine till date

title: Mahasweta Devi’s best books: grandson Tathagata Bhattacharya recommends five must-reads
content: The writer-activist once told her grandson that she was appreciated for the wrong reasons. Recalling that conversation, he lists five works that he believes are the most powerful

title: Lucknow activist demands Donor Assurance Certificate from Ram Mandir Trust
content: Lucknow-based social activist Amitabh Thakur has put forth a demand to the Ram Temple Trust to ensure that the required transparency and accountability are achieved and public faith is restored

title: AMMA crisis: In letter to members, Shwetha Menon says panel to ‘rectify lapses caused by lack of experience’
content: Shwetha Menon, who has reversed her decision to step down as AMMA president, says executive committee led by her is ‘duty-bound' to move forward with more responsibility

title: Two hurt as fishing boat capsizes near Visakhapatnam Fishing Harbour; all seven survive
content: The vessel overturned in strong currents and wedged between rocks near the Fishing Harbour; two injured fishermen are being treated at the KGH, less than a fortnight after six others were lost off Gangavaram

title: Mahasweta Devi at 100: Why her greatest legacy lies in the Adivasi movements she helped build
content: In Mahasweta Devi’s birth centenary year, and a decade since her death, a look at the writer’s enduring impact on grassroots movements through literature and activism

title: Mahasweta Devi at 100: Naveen Kishore remembers a life of writing as activism
content: Naveen Kishore, Mahasweta Devi’s long-time friend and poet-publisher, reflects on the phenomenon that she was and her literature of resistance

title: Akhilesh hits out at BJP over demolition notice to University linked with Azam Khan
content: The Rashtriya Janata Dal (RJD) Rajya Sabha MP Manoj Jha said the regime that seeks to destroy universities, whether with bulldozers or through administrative repression, doesn’t merely demolish buildings; it attacks thought, conscience, and the future.

title: Provident Fund interest credited to all 34 crore accounts on July 15
content: The Union Finance Ministry had recently approved the recommendation of the EPFO’s Central Board of Trustees to provide interest at the rate of 8.25%; a senior EPFO official said the credited interest will be visible to all members when they open passbook for 2025-26.

title: BJP’s statewide protests against Omar’s J&K govt. to coincide with NC’s Jantar Mantar sit-in on Statehood
content: The BJP is preparing to organise protests over the J&K government’s outsourcing of jobs and will assemble outside the Civil Secretariat in Srinagar on July 20

title: CM Suvendu joins Rath Yatra in Kolkata; several BJP leaders join festivities
content: Suvendu Adhikari reiterates their plan to hand over mid-day meals to ISKCON, even as no official notification has been issued so far

title: Sauhardya Karnataka stages protest over NEET paper leak, expresses solidarity with Sonam Wangchuk
content: The environmentalist is on an indefinite hunger strike demanding the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan over the alleged NEET examination paper leak

title: What’s new in the draft CAFE-III norms? | Explained
content: The latest draft leaves most of the April proposal unchanged, suggesting the government has broadly stuck to the compromise it arrived at after months of intense negotiations between manufacturers of small cars and larger SUVs.

title: Flu, H1N1 rampant in Kerala; 4,405 cases of influenza reported
content: Surveillance of all influenza-like illnesses and severe acute respiratory infections has been intensified across districts and the treatment protocol and guidelines have been revisited

title: As Wangchuk’s health worsens, Delhi High Court orders regular monitoring
content: Wangchuk has lost over 9 kg after starting hunger strike and his body is consuming muscles and his organs may be next, says doctor; govt. assures High Court of necessary medical intervention

title: 12 COVID cases, 4 deaths reported in A.P. since June 26, but no need to worry, says health official
content: There has been no cluster outbreak, says the Health Secretary; describing the situation as periodic cyclical viral circulations, expert says that the infections may blow over by August end

title: After ambushes, CRPF tells forces in Manipur to use armoured vehicles, avoid sudden deployment
content: An official said the forces have been told to take action against armed miscreants and “anyone found with a gun is to be arrested or fired upon, if they do not surrender”

title: ITC hotels announces acquisition of 130 key operating hotel in Ahmedabad’s commercial hub
content: The addition of this hotel would further strengthen the company’s owned asset portfolio in Ahmedabad by straddling across all the branded hospitality market segments and price points, the company said.

title: Rights body flags illegal coal trade near Assam-Arunachal border
content: The Tinsukia unit of International Human Rights Council says coal is illegally extracted and collected from abandoned Coal India mines and transported through an alleged “entry system”

title: Five child marriages stopped through court-issued injunctions on a single day in Rajasthan’s Sikar
content: Issued by the court of the Judicial Magistrate under Section 13(1) of the Prohibition of Child Marriage Act, an injunction order empowers the authorities to proactively prevent a child marriage before it occurs

title: Karnataka asks Centre to declare drought situation as ‘calamity of national significance’
content: Preliminary assessments indicate that nearly 80% of the sown crop has been lost in several affected areas due to inadequate rainfall and persistently high temperatures: Parameshwara

title: How India’s first hydrogen-powered train works | Explained
content: Unlike conventional diesel locomotives that burn fuel to generate mechanical power, a hydrogen train carries a small power plant onboard in the form of a Proton Exchange Membrane (PEM) fuel cell

title: Why the numbers matter in the renewed push for  delimitation | Explained
content: For the Opposition, the immediate objective is to keep the NDA below the 360-vote threshold; even if the ruling alliance emerges as the single largest bloc by a comfortable margin, failing to secure two-thirds support would once again prevent the Constitution amendment from being passed.

title: Draft Apartment Bill gives flat owners stronger rights over common areas
content: In the proposed law, ownership of the land and common areas will remain with the flat owners collectively, and not with the association itself

title: ₹5 lakh bribe leads to arrest of MD of Bengaluru-based Karnataka Antibiotics and Pharmaceuticals Limited by CBI
content: Karnataka Antibiotics and Pharmaceuticals Limited (KAPL) Managing Director Anurag Danayak was allegedly caught red-handed taking the bribe from a company that is a service agent of KAPL, according to a CBI spokesperson

title: Kochi Corporation orders probe into spending of ₹6.32 crore for streetlight maintenance in 2022-23
content: A report on expenditure towards streetlight repair and maintenance tabled in council; a survey is to be conducted to verify number of LED lights that CSML claimed to have installed under City LED Street Light project last year and assess number of old lights that remain to be replaced, says Mayor

title: Avoid deploying Indian sailors on vessels moving via Strait of Hormuz: DGMA to shipping companies
content: The advisory came after the attacks this week on two vessels, in which 30 Indian seafarers were among a combined crew of 46

title: Daily ridership of women passengers on KSRTC Ordinary buses increase from 6.48 lakh to 12.12 lakh, says Kerala Minister
content: Despite the roll-out of the scheme on Ordinary buses, number of male passengers using these services has remained almost constant. This indicates that increase in women passengers has not come at the expense of other passengers, says C.P. John

title: Chhattisgarh passes Bill to boost business
content: The Bill brings 43 services offered by eight state departments under the risk-based approval framework, with provisions to add more services after approval by the Executive Council

title: Govt. lists five Bills for Monsoon Session, no mention of delimitation legislation
content: Government to table Prevention of Insults to National Honour (Amendment) Bill that will make the intentional insult of, or disruption during, the singing of Vande Mataram a punishable offence

title: NEET-UG 2026 results: 11.21 lakh candidates qualify in re-test; women outshine men
content: Two candidates, Aryan Gupta from Punjab and Panshul Bansal from Haryana, secured the highest score of 715 (out of 720 marks) in the re-examination; 19 candidates crossed the 700-mark threshold; of the top 138 candidates (scores above 690), 93% cracked the exam on their first attempt, with 99% of them aged between 17 and 19 years

title: Rot in KPSC recruitment runs deep
content: The recent scandal to hit the recruitment process in the Karnataka State Public Service Commission, with the Chairperson facing allegations of nepotism, is only the latest in a series over the last few decades. Sharath S. Srivatsa takes an overview of the system

title: Six murders, a dead suspect and a chilling trail gone cold
content: On July 10, six persons were killed across two homes in Daivalaguda, a peaceful farming village near Hyderabad. With investigations under way, the case raises uncomfortable questions about police procedure, anticipatory bail, victim protection and whether the tragedy could have been prevented

title: Former Maoists trade years of conflict for livelihood and a stable future
content: For many former Maoists, surrender only marks the beginning of a long journey. While they attempt to rebuild their lives through farming and family, rehabilitation remains a work in progress. Many continue to grapple with unemployment, inadequate skills, and challenges of reintegration, writes T. Appalanaidu

title: ‘Kafir screenshot’ case: order on SIT plea to cancel accused’s bail today
content: The Kozhikode Sessions Court is also expected to pronounce its order on the anticipatory bail plea filed by another DYFI leader, Ribesh Ramakrishnan, in connection with the case

title: SC dismisses plea by Karnataka Legislative Council Deputy Chairman against recounting of votes
content: The appeal was heard by a three-judge comprising Chief Justice of India Surya Kant, Justice Vipul M. Pancholi, and Justice Joymalya Bagchi

title: Trinamool MP Samirul Islam calls on family who was pushed into Bangladesh
content: Four Bengali migrants from Birbhum who were detained by Delhi Police last year and pushed into Bangladesh were brought back to India on July 8, 2026 after a year-long legal battle. It was Samirul Islam who took up the cause of the migrant workers and provided help to the family

title: Dates with devotion: The division in Puri’s Rath Yatra celebrations
content: The Jagannath Temple in Puri holds its Rath Yatra every year on a date prescribed by its centuries-old ritual calendar. A branch of ISKCON, however, stages international events of the procession on dates that differ from the original, citing practical reasons. The divergence has sparked a controversy, with many in Puri viewing it as a challenge to the temple’s authority and its religious identity, finds Satyasundar Barik

title: Higher education Bill may lead to concentration of powers in single central regulator: House panel
content: The Bill was introduced in the Lok Sabha in December last year, following which it was referred to a joint committee of Parliament

title: BMTC plans higher non-fare revenue with QR code-based ads on bus seatbacks
content: The proposed QR displays will measure 10 cm x 10 cm and will be placed on the rear side of passenger seats, according to officials.

title: Supreme Court judgments say state has a duty to preserve life of person on indefinite fast without disrupting right to dissent
content: The Union government has responded to activist Sonam Wanghuk’s ongoing fasting at Jantar Mantar with silence for the past 19 days even as an alarmed Delhi High Court asserted on July 16 that the ‘life of any citizen is precious’

title: Congress must pass 'Voter ID Save America Act': Trump in his address to United States

title: Trump claims Obama, Biden benefited from ‘cover-up’ of China’s 2020 election meddling
content: president Donald Trump accused officials of concealing evidence of alleged interference . he also claimed documents linked to former president Barack Obama had also been found among material that was supposed to be destroyed . recently discovered burn bags contained information provided by president Barack Hussein Obama .

title: "Weak From Outside, Strong Inside": Sonam Wangchuk As Fast Enters Day 20
content: The 59-year-old Wangchuk, who is demanding the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan over the alleged NEET paper leak, has urged people to ensure a massive turnout for the Cockroach Janta Party's (CJP) proposed march to parliament on July 20.

title: India Gets Its 1st Hydrogen-Powered Train, Will Run On 89 Km Route In Haryana
content: The technology will use hydrogen fuel cells to produce electricity, making it a cleaner alternative to diesel trains.

title: SpaceX Short Sellers Make $4 Billion: Why Investors Are Betting Against Elon Musk
content: Despite the recent pressure on its market valuation, analysts believe SpaceX remains the global leader in commercial space launches.

title: China's Xi Says AI Should Not Be Dominated By One Nation
content: AI has become a strategic pillar of China's industrial policy, driven by state investment aimed at building a domestic ecosystem, from chip production to consumer use.

title: Jagan Mohan Reddy Launched AI 'Super App' To Connect With Party Cadre
content: 'Jagan 2.0 Super App' allows party cadre to report local issues, upload complaints of alleged political harassment, access live party events, participate in campaigns, receive organisational updates, and communicate directly with the party leadership.

title: 973-Page Chargsheet Filed In Delhi IRS Officer's Daughter Rape, Murder Case
content: During interrogation, the accused Rahul Meena allegedly told investigators he went to the house "just to ask for money" and that crime "just happened."

title: UP To Tamil Nadu: Man Begins Cross-Country Foot March To Meet Vijay
content: Carrying the national flag throughout his journey, Aman said his mission is inspired by his admiration for the Chief Minister's governance, development initiatives, and welfare programmes.

title: US Immigration Officer In Maine Shooting Has History Of Violent Behavio: Report

title: Andy Burnham: 'King Of The North' To Become Next UK PM
content: Andy Burnham's elevation to the top job follows a successful nine-year stint as Greater Manchester mayor, during which Burnham transformed himself from former government minister to a political outsider.

title: Trump-Backed Gaza Recovery Plan Now Just A Pilot Project: Report
